courseleaf.title: Native American Studies, Minor
courseleaf.informationtext: The Native American Studies minor provides an interdisciplinary introduction to the Native experience in the Americas through coursework in history, literature, art, performance, languages, values, philosophy, religion, current events, political economy, and environment.
courseleaf.requirementstext:
Course List
Code Title Units
Choose one lower division Native American Studies course.4
Native American Studies (NAS) courses.
Choose five upper division Native American Studies courses.20
Native American Studies (NAS) courses.
UC Davis students should take at least two UC Davis courses in fulfillment of the minor requirements. With approval of the minor advisor, the remainder of the minor requirements can be fulfilled via UC Davis courses and/or NAS/AIS/NAIS courses offered via UC Online from any campus in the UC system.
Students from other campuses may also consult their departmental advisors about counting UC Online courses towards their regular degrees if NAS, AIS, or NAIS majors or minors are offered at their universities.
Total Units24
courseleaf.quickviewtext: The Native American Studies minor provides an interdisciplinary introduction to the Native experience in the Americas through coursework in history, literature, art, performance, languages, values, philosophy, religion, current events, political-economic, and the environment.
